faults.description: Confirm signal not defined in Taxi-to-Driver API. After a driver-submitted order, the central sends Confirm to the taxi module (3.4.1), but 3.2.2 provides no Confirm signal for the module to forward this confirmation to the driver.

No signal to inform the taxi that no cars are available at the requested time. Variant 4b requires the driver to be notified so a new order with a different pick-up time can be requested, but no such signal is defined in 3.4.1.

Order_struct contains no priority field. Task 2 states that orders can be prioritized and some customers are more important than others, but the data structure provides no attribute to represent order priority.

No signal for sending a time estimate to the operator when an order is very close in time. Variant 3b requires the system to present how long it will take for a car to be available, but no corresponding signal exists in 3.3.2.

LoginSignal defined without parameters. Task 3 requires the terminal to send driver identification information to the central at login, but LoginSignal carries no parameters and therefore cannot convey driver ID.
LoggedIn(integer) carries only the taxi number. Task 3 requires driver information to be transmitted to the central, but the LoggedIn signal has no driver identification parameter.
LoginFailed missing from Central-to-Taxi API. Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid card and notify the taxi, but no LoginFailed signal is defined in 3.4.1.

ZoneInfo absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Task 6 requires the central to start sending overview information to the car after login, but ZoneInfo is not defined as a Central-to-Taxi signal, making this impossible.

Confirm signal not present in Taxi-to-Driver API (3.2.2). Task 4 requires the central to confirm a received order to the driver, but the taxi module cannot forward the Confirm from 3.4.1 to the driver without this signal.

No signal from Central to Taxi for order cancellation after 2-minute timeout. Variant 3b requires automatic cancellation if the driver neither accepts nor rejects within 2 minutes, but no cancellation signal is defined in 3.4.1.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.6 Taxi: Driving a customer
faults.description: No signal to communicate the Waiting-for-Customer state to the central. After accepting an order (UC 1.5 Task 6), the car must enter this state before meter start, but no corresponding state signal is defined in the Taxi-to-Central API.

Position(integer, integer) missing a time parameter. Task 8 requires the driver to send both arrival zone and expected arrival time to the central when approaching the destination, but Position only carries taxi number and zone number.

faults.use_case: 1.7 Central: Handle incoming alarms / 1.8 Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: No alarm trigger signal defined in Taxi-to-Central API. UC 1.8 Tasks 1-2 require the taxi to immediately alert the central and open a voice channel when the driver activates the alarm, but 3.4.2 defines no alarm notification signal.

Position signal transmits zone number, not exact coordinates. Task 3 requires the taxi to send exact coordinates every 30 seconds during an alarm event, but Position(integer, integer) provides only a coarse zone number.

ZoneInfo absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Task 2 requires the central to send overview information to the car terminal in response to an Overview request (3.2.1), but no ZoneInfo signal exists in 3.4.1.

No notification signal to operator when a manually dispatched order is rejected by the car. Variant 4b requires the operator to be informed to reassign the order, but no rejection notification is defined in 3.3.2.

Text_Message signal cannot address a group of cars. Task 3 requires the operator to choose a specific car, a group of cars, or all cars as destination. The signal supports one car (by number) or all (0), but provides no group mechanism.

No text message acknowledgment signal in Taxi-to-Central API. Task 4 requires each car receiving a text message to automatically acknowledge receipt to the central, but no such acknowledgment signal is defined in 3.4.2.
No signal to notify the operator of a failed text message delivery to a specific car. Variant 4b explicitly requires this notification, but no corresponding signal exists in 3.3.2.

faults.use_case: 1.12 Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: No operator login signal defined in Central-Operator API. UC 1.12 requires operators to log in with user identification and password, but 3.3.1 defines no signal for submitting credentials to the system.

MSC 4.1 missing Central-to-Taxi Confirm after Ack (step 8). Task 4 requires the central to confirm the accepted order to the driver, but no such step is depicted in MSC 4.1 after the central receives the Ack.

faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal missing from communication protocol Table 1. The Confirm signal is sent from Central to Taxi over the radio link (3.4.1) but has no entry in Table 1, so it cannot be encoded for transmission.

No signal for the driver to request properties (3.2.1) and no signal from Central to Taxi to deliver property information (3.4.1). UC 1.16 requires both request and response, but neither signal is defined.

No signal for updating driver properties in Central-Operator API. UC 1.18 requires the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties, but 3.3.1 defines no signal to support this interaction.
